title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with gum pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tannins present in sage tincture also help to numb the affected area, providing relief from pain and sensitivity. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to gum pain. By using sage tinctures, individuals can effectively alleviate gum pain and promote healthy gums and a refreshing smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum pain.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in sage tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidant properties, including its high concentration of polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against gum infections and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sage leaves, measuring out 1 cup of dried or 2 cups of fresh material for the tincture recipe.
Chop the harvested sage into smaller pieces and combine with 1 pint of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds from the herb.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, squeezing as much liquid as possible from the plant material.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years, taking 20-30 drops sublingually as needed for gum pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood sage tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The herbal extract also has antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, wood sage's analgesic properties help numb the affected area, providing quick relief from sharp pains and aching sensations. By soothing and calming the gums, wood sage tinctures provide natural and effective relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds: Wood sage tincture contains bioactive compounds like thujone, borneol, and camphor that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and combat infections in the gums.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The tincture's active ingredients, such as camphor and thujone, have a direct impact on pain pathways, reducing pain perception and providing temporary relief from gum discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Wood sage tincture contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals in the mouth, protecting gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, which can contribute to gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, roots of Wood Sage help alleviate inflammation and pain associated with gum disease.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of Wood Sage contain bioactive compounds that reduce bacterial growth and combat oral infections causing gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium polium leaves and stems in the morning when dew is still present.
Chop 20 grams of dried or 40 grams of fresh teucrium polium material into small pieces for steeping.
Combine chopped teucrium polium with 400 milliliters of high-proof grain alcohol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days, shaking the jar daily to extract tannins and flavonoids.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and store the finished tincture at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in cornflower have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and pain. Additionally, cornflower's antibacterial properties help combat plaque and prevent further infection, promoting healthy gums and reducing pain caused by abscesses or cavities. By addressing the underlying causes of gum pain, herbal cornflower tinctures provide effective relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cornflower contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain caused by inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in cornflower tincture, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Cornflower's antimicrobial compounds, like rosmarinic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, leading to pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to soothe oral mucosa and reduce inflammation associated with gum pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gum pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried centaurea cyanus flowers in sufficient quantity for desired yield of tincture.
Combine 1 part dried centaurea cyanus flowers with 3 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking jar daily to ensure uniform extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store prepared tincture in the refrigerator at 39°f to 45°f and use within 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacteria that can cause infections, leading to chronic gum pain. Additionally, pot marigold's analgesic properties provide relief from acute toothaches and sensitivity. By soothing and calming the gums, this natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective solution for alleviating gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antibacterial properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold have antibacterial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and disease.

Wound-healing activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds also promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and reducing scarring, all of which can help to alleviate gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.

Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a compound called saponin, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, helping to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers for a total of 1 cup or 250 grams.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cool place and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to soothe irritated gum tissues and alleviate sensitivity. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat gum infections that can cause pain and inflammation. By using herbal licorice tinctures, individuals can experience natural relief from gum discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease gum pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains various flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe gum irritation and prevent further inflammation.

Triterpenoids: The triterpenoid fraction of licorice root has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce gum bleeding and swelling, making it an effective ingredient for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh licorice root and dry it for two weeks or purchase dried licorice root from a reputable supplier.
Measure out one part dried licorice root and mix it with three parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar every other day to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles and label them with ingredients and preparation date, storing them in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid and cinnamate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. When taken internally as a tincture, these compounds can help soothe gum tissue and reduce swelling, providing relief from toothaches, gum recession, and other gum-related discomforts. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties may also help combat gum infections and promote healthy oral flora, further contributing to its effectiveness in addressing gum pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidants neutralize free radicals, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cinnamon contains cinnamaldehyde, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and inflammation.

Natural analgesic properties: Ceylon cinnamon's tincture has been shown to exhibit natural analgesic properties, which can help numb or reduce the sensation of pain in the gums, providing temporary relief.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the Ceylon cinnamon plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for gum pain due to their high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Barks: The outer bark of the Ceylon cinnamon plant is used to create medicinal tinctures that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the gums, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ceylon cinnamon bark and dry it in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to preserve its medicinal properties.
Grind 10 grams of dried ceylon cinnamon bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground ceylon cinnamon powder with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the powder is completely covered by the liquid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for the active compounds to infuse into the tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer it to a glass dropper bottle, using 20-30 drops as needed up to three times daily to alleviate gum pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's antimicrobial agents also combat bacteria that can cause infection and further exacerbate gum pain. Additionally, Kansas coneflower contains compounds that have natural analgesic and antiseptic properties, providing relief from sensitivity and soreness. By addressing the root causes of gum pain, this herbal remedy promotes a healthy and pain-free mouth.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Kansas Coneflower, such as cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to gum pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Kansas Coneflower contains antibacterial and antifungal compounds that can help combat infections in the mouth, such as gum disease-causing bacteria, reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Immune system support: The tincture's immune-boosting properties may help stimulate the body's natural defenses to fight off oral pathogens, including those responsible for gum disease and related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the Kansas coneflower have been traditionally used to treat gum pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids that help reduce pain and swelling associated with gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall after blooming and dry them thoroughly for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried echinacea roots into small pieces and combine with 80% vodka or everclear to create a 1:4 root-to-solvent ratio mixture.
Place the root mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily for 7-10 minutes each day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids and store the tincture in the refrigerator at 40°f to 50°f (4°c to 10°c).
Label the tincture with the date prepared ingredients used and dosage instructions and take 20-30 drops sublingually twice daily as needed for gum pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The herb's flavonoids and hyperforin also have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate toothaches, sensitivity, and pain caused by gum recession or inflammation. Additionally, St John's Wort's antimicrobial properties may help combat gum infections that contribute to pain and discomfort, providing relief from irritated and inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort tincture helps combat free radicals and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation and pain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and polyphenols present in St. John's Wort tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum disease or other oral infections.

Antimicrobial activity: St. John's Wort tincture exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections in the mouth, such as gingivitis or periodontitis, that may be causing gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in mid-summer morning dew for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material with paper towels and air circulation for 24 hours.
Chop the dried st john's wort into smaller pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine one part chopped st john's wort with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four weeks shaking daily then strain and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, including salicin and flavonoids, work to alleviate inflammation and soothe irritated gum tissue. Additionally, guelder rose tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain. By addressing these underlying causes of discomfort, herbal guelder rose tinctures provide natural relief from gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Guelder Rose tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.
Anodyne properties: Guelder Rose contains anodynes, which are substances that numb or dull pain, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and pain.
Barks: Contain astringent properties, which help reduce bleeding gums and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried guelder rose leaves and flowers in the summer months.
Combine 1 part guelder rose plant material with 1 part vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate clean container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, including thujone and sesquiterpene lactones, work to alleviate inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and swelling. Additionally, wormwood's antimicrobial properties help combat gum infections, further contributing to its ability to ease gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial properties can help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to gum pain, allowing for a faster recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum pain, making it easier to manage chronic conditions like gingivitis and periodontitis.

Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidant properties can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress in the gums, which can exacerbate gum pain and damage tissue over time.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood are used to create a potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial tincture that targets gum inflammation and infection.
Barks: The barks of wormwood contain sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic properties that help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are dry and fragrant.
Dry the plant material thoroughly using a food dehydrator or by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags for 7-10 days.
Combine one part dried wormwood with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction, before straining through cheesecloth into a clean container.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass dropper bottles and store them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation, using 20-30 drops as needed up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural analgesic properties. The methyl salicylate found in wintergreen has a similar composition to aspirin, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When applied topically as a tincture, the active compounds in wintergreen are absorbed quickly into the affected area, providing quick relief from gum discomfort. Additionally, wintergreen's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on sensitive gum tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for managing gum pain.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This key compound in wintergreen has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing gum pain and swelling by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and inflammation.

Glycosides: Wintergreen's glycosides have been shown to exhibit antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infections and reduce the risk of tooth decay or gum disease, both common causes of gum pain.

Astringent properties: The astringent properties of wintergreen tincture can help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums, making it useful for treating gingivitis and periodontal disease, which are major contributors to gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that effectively treats gum pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the wintergreen plant are rich in menthol and other volatile oils that provide analgesic and antiseptic properties to soothe gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems at dawn when they are most potent.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and weigh out 100 grams for the batch.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to amber glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like parthenolide and sesquiterpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the gums and surrounding tissues. When used as a tincture, these active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from gum pain and swelling. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing chronic gum pain or sensitivity caused by conditions such as gingivitis or periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum disease or injury.
Antioxidant properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Joe Pye Weed help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum recession and pain.
Pain-relieving compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains salicylic acid, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce pain and discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Joe Pye weed have been traditionally used to treat oral health issues and provide relief from gum pain.
Barks: The bark of Joe Pye weed contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems, weighing approximately one pound for this recipe.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to ensure maximum surface area exposure during the extraction process.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, using a 1:5 ratio by weight of herb to solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate efficient solvent penetration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in goldenrod, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum inflammation. By soothing the gums and surrounding tissues, goldenrod tincture can provide fast relief from throbbing pain and sensitivity, making it a natural and effective solution for individuals experiencing gum pain caused by periodontal disease, toothaches, or dental procedures.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of goldenrod tincture, including its ability to inhibit bacterial growth, can help combat gum infections and prevent further damage, reducing pain and promoting healing.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that can help alleviate gum inflammation and pain.
Seeds: The seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat oral health issues, including gum pain, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried solidago virgaurea leaves and stems in the morning for maximum potency.
Combine one part of the plant material with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle after the steeping period.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place, using one to two droppers full (1/2 teaspoon) three times a day for gum pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's high concentration of antioxidants also helps to combat oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment. Additionally, dandelion's natural antibacterial qualities inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease, further reducing pain and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Pour 80-100 proof vodka over the herb mixture ensuring it is completely covered.
Label the jar and date it then store it in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing the tincture to mature.
After the maturation period strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the resulting herbal extract into dark glass dropper bottles and label them with ingredients dosages and instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids and terpenes present in yarrow extract help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving gum pain caused by tooth decay, gingivitis, or other oral health issues. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 70% ethanol vodka or neutral grain spirits as the solvent in a ratio of 1 part plant material to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it with the date and contents for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with gum pain because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and leaves, using scissors or pruning shears to collect fresh material.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the clean plant material into small pieces, ensuring uniformity for efficient extraction, and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 part althaea officinalis material with 2 parts vodka in the glass jar, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids, and label as finished tincture ready for use.

Combine one part dried rosa rugosa flowers with three parts high-proof vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the plant compounds to infuse into the solvent.

As a result, many people find that using herbal lemon balm tinctures helps to alleviate gum pain and promote overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum pain.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm may help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, such as periodontitis, which can contribute to gum pain.

Anxiolytic and sedative: Lemon balm's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common factors that exacerbate gum pain, allowing for a more relaxing and healing environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm gum inflammation, making them a great choice for creating tinctures to alleviate gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high amounts of rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day for maximum potency.
Chop 250 grams of fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves into fine pieces.
Combine chopped melissa officinalis with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter after eight weeks into another clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant effects can help to calm gum tissue spasms, reducing sensitivity and soreness. This natural remedy can be easily administered by placing a few drops of the tincture under the tongue or adding it to water for a soothing rinse.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant agents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum damage and pain, promoting healthy tissue and reducing inflammation.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for gum pain, allowing the individual to relax and reduce their discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing infections.
Flowers: Containing azulene, an essential oil with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe gum pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the fresh flowers by spreading them out on paper bags or clean cloths.
Combine one part of the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with three parts of high-proof vodka.
Steep the mixture for four to six weeks, shaking the container daily, before filtering and straining.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles and use within two years or after two weeks of opening.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum pain are concentrated liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients, specifically designed to alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with gum disease. These natural remedies work by reducing swelling, fighting off bacterial infections, and soothing soreness in the gums. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with gum pain include peppermint, chamomile, and myrrh, which can be applied topically or taken orally. By using these tinctures, individuals suffering from gum pain can experience relief from chronic discomfort, improve their oral health, and regain confidence in their overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum pain with herbal tinctures is to start by mixing a few drops of peppermint oil or clove tincture with water to create a mouthwash. Gargle for 30 seconds before spitting it out and repeat every hour as needed. For severe pain, try using a tincture made from ginger or arnica to reduce inflammation and numb the area. Always dilute the tinctures in water according to instructions before applying them to the gums.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are various oral and dental issues, such as toothaches, abscesses, and mouth ulcers. Herbal tinctures like Calendula, Tea Tree, and Echinacea have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. Additionally, tinctures of Ginger, Peppermint, and Sage may also provide relief from gum pain by reducing inflammation and easing discomfort.
